remediates: codex §9 [P2]#1 — non-deterministic Hamming fanout: np.argpartition picked an ARBITRARY subset of docs tied at the Kth Hamming distance (ubiquitous with 1-bit vectors); that candidate set is f32-reranked and feeds the projected_eu7 >= 0.90 gate, so a near-floor candidate could pass/fail on an unpinned tie

codex §9 [P2]#2 — vector cache reused {model}.docs.npy/{model}.queries.npy on ROW COUNT alone, so a different resolved revision / changed HF_HOME / same-sized-but-different input set silently fed STALE vectors into every gate metric while the output still reported the current model_revisions
commits: bb919817 test(0.8.3/Slice 15a fix-2): RED — deterministic Hamming fanout + cache identity guard
aa95a087 fix(0.8.3/Slice 15a fix-2): stable Hamming fanout tie-break + revision/input cache sidecar (codex §9 P2x2)

p2_1_fix.what: projected_eu7's per-query fanout selection at the former line ~388 changed from `np.argpartition(ham, kk - 1)[:kk]` to `np.argsort(ham, kind="stable")[:kk]` — lexicographic (hamming_distance, doc_index) ordering, so docs tied at the Kth Hamming distance are broken deterministically by index instead of by argpartition's arbitrary partition order.
p2_1_fix.semantics_preserved: Fanout K is unchanged (still kk == min(PROJECTED_EU7_K=192, n_docs)); only the tie determinism changed, not the value of K. The kk>=n_docs short-circuit (cand_idx = arange) is untouched.
p2_1_fix.other_sites_verified: The other top-k sites (cosine_topk line ~227, BM25Okapi.topk ~335, projected_eu7 exact-truth ~370, the f32 rerank ~391) already use np.argsort(..., kind="stable") and are deterministic; left as-is. Line ~388 argpartition was the only defect.
p2_1_fix.red_demonstration: test_projected_eu7_deterministic_hamming_fanout builds 14 docs with ham layout [0,0,3,0,2,0,1,1,1,1,0,0,0,0] (q=all-ones) where numpy argpartition(kk=9) keeps doc index 8 and DROPS index 6 among the ham=1 boundary ties, while stable index order keeps index 6. Index 6 is the global f32-exact top-1 (dot 29.99). Current argpartition path -> projected_eu7 = 0.0 (RED); stable fanout -> 1.0 (GREEN). Verified repeatable run-to-run.

p2_2_fix.what: embed_model now writes a {model}.meta.json sidecar (via new _cache_meta + _atomic_write_json) capturing {revision, corpus_hash, n_docs, n_queries, pooling, dim}. On --resume the warm .npy cache is reused ONLY if the sidecar EXISTS and equals the current expected meta (and row counts still match); a missing or mismatched or unparseable sidecar => re-embed (fail-safe).
p2_2_fix.fail_safe: Reuse requires doc_npy.exists() and qry_npy.exists() and meta_npy.exists() and on_disk == expected_meta; json.JSONDecodeError/OSError on the sidecar => on_disk=None => re-embed. The new sidecar is written atomically (tmp + os.replace) alongside the atomic .npy saves.
p2_2_fix.expected_full_reembed: The existing warm /tmp/fdb-s15a-cache-full/*.npy have NO sidecar, so the orchestrator's next --full will re-embed them. That is correct and expected; no back-fill of the old cache was attempted (and that cache was left untouched).
p2_2_fix.red_demonstration: test_cache_sidecar_revision_mismatch_reembeds writes stale .npy (matching row counts) + a sidecar pinning revision REV_OLD, then calls embed_model with cfg.revision=REV_A and a monkeypatched fresh-vector embed_texts. Current code reuses the stale .npy on row count alone (RED); fixed code detects the revision mismatch and returns the freshly re-embedded vectors. test_cache_sidecar_match_reuses asserts a fully-matching sidecar reuses the warm cache with NO embed_texts call.

tests.gate_unit: FATHOMDB_TESTS_NO_REBUILD=1 PYTHONPATH=src/python .venv/bin/python -m pytest src/python/tests/test_s15a_embedder_probe.py -q -m 'not integration' -p no:cacheprovider => 12 passed, 4 deselected (was 9 passed pre-fix; +3 new: test_projected_eu7_deterministic_hamming_fanout, test_cache_sidecar_revision_mismatch_reembeds, test_cache_sidecar_match_reuses).
tests.red_confirmed: At the RED commit (bb919817), 2 of the 3 new tests FAILED on baseline code: test_projected_eu7_deterministic_hamming_fanout (argpartition -> 0.0 != expected 1.0) and test_cache_sidecar_revision_mismatch_reembeds (stale .npy reused on row count). test_cache_sidecar_match_reuses passed on baseline (the reuse path already existed) and documents the match contract.
tests.integration_cpu: No new integration tests; the 4 existing torch integration tests remain deselected by -m 'not integration' (gate venv has no torch).

pyright: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 informations on both changed files (pyright 1.1.409, PYTHONPATH=src/python canonical .venv python)
determinism_confirmed: projected_eu7 is now repeatable run-to-run: the fanout candidate set is the stable (hamming, index) ordering, so no gate-feeding metric depends on an unpinned argpartition tie.
cache_guard_confirmed: Cache reuse now requires a sidecar matching (revision, corpus_hash, n_docs, n_queries, pooling, dim); a different revision or input set re-embeds instead of serving stale vectors.
guardrails: No GPU/airlock; offline + $0; did NOT run --full (orchestrator-owned); did NOT touch /tmp/fdb-s15a-cache-full/ or dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-s15a-embedder.json; did NOT modify decision_rule_083.py or the gold; no merge/push; no agent spawn; no maturin develop; stayed inside the /tmp/fdb-slice-15a-6b8503da worktree.
notes: The cache key (.npy filenames) is still the model NAME; the sidecar is the identity guard, not a key change. This means the next --full re-embeds the sidecar-less warm vectors once, after which subsequent --resume runs reuse correctly.

argpartition vs argsort divergence is numpy-implementation-specific but deterministic for a fixed input/version; the RED was confirmed on the installed numpy and the GREEN assertion (== 1.0) holds regardless of numpy version since the fix uses argsort.
next_step_for_orchestrator: codex §9 re-review on the branch; then cherry-pick. On the next orchestrator --full (eu-0 venv + HF_HOME) the sidecar-less warm cache will be re-embedded once and a {model}.meta.json written per model; projected_eu7 is now tie-deterministic.
